title: High-Molecular-Weight gDNA Extraction Protocol from Moss Gametophytes
author: Lei Zhu
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bud2ns8e

steps.title: Harvest 3-week-old moss gametophyte tissue
steps.instruction: Harvest 3-week-old moss gametophyte tissue.

steps.title: Carefully remove rhizoids from leafy gametophytes. IMPORTANT
steps.instruction: Carefully remove rhizoids from leafy gametophytes. IMPORTANT: Rhizoids mixed in the green leafy tissue will cause pigment co-precipitated with DNA. Be careful to remove rhizoids from leafy gametophytes.

steps.title: Grind leafy gametophytes to fine powder...
steps.instruction: Grind leafy gametophytes to fine powder in liquid nitrogen using the mortar and pestle. IMPORTANT: Grinding moss leafy gametophytes to fine powder is very important. If the powder is coarse, the efficiency of extraction will be much lower than expected.

steps.title: Transfer the ground powder to a 50 mL conical tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the ground powder to a 50 mL conical tube.

steps.title: Put the conical tube in a...
steps.instruction: Put the conical tube in a -80 °C freezer with cap open until all liquid nitrogen evaporate.

steps.title: Add 10 mL pre-heated 2x CTAB...
steps.instruction: Add 10 mL pre-heated 2x CTAB buffer to the 50 mL conical tube that contains ground gametophyte powder.

steps.title: Close the cap and shake the...
steps.instruction: Close the cap and shake the tube vigorously to mix CTAB buffer and gametophyte powder.

steps.title: Incubate the conical tube in 65...
steps.instruction: Incubate the conical tube in 65 °C water bath for 00:45:00 .

steps.title: Shake the tube to mix buffer...
steps.instruction: Shake the tube to mix buffer and gametophyte powder every 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Take the conical tube out of...
steps.instruction: Take the conical tube out of the water bath and cool down to Room temperature .

steps.title: Add 10 mL chloroform
steps.instruction: Add 10 mL chloroform: isoamyl alcohol to the conical tube. Mix well.

steps.title: Then put the tube to a...
steps.instruction: Then put the tube to a rotator for 00:20:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1000 x g, °C,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1000 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 at Room temperature to separate phases.

steps.title: Transfer the upper supernatant to a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the upper supernatant to a new 50 mL conical tube. Add 1 mL 10% CTAB and 10 mL chloroform: isoamyl alcohol to the new tube. Shake well.

steps.title: Incubate the conical tube at Room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate the conical tube at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge the conical tube at 1000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the conical tube at 1000 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 to separate phases.

steps.title: Transfer the upper aqueous supernatant to a new conical tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the upper aqueous supernatant to a new conical tube. Add 10 mL isopropanol and mix well.

steps.title: Centrifuge the conical tube at 1000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the conical tube at 1000 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 to precipitate the pellet.

steps.title: Decant the supernatant
steps.instruction: Decant the supernatant. Air dry the pellet.

steps.title: Dissolve the pellet in 4 mL TE buffer
steps.instruction: Dissolve the pellet in 4 mL TE buffer. Tap the pellet gently until it dissolves completely.

steps.title: Centrifuge the tube at 1000 x g, °C, for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tube at 1000 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to a new 50 mL tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to a new 50 mL tube. Discard pellets.

steps.title: Incubate the tube On ice for 00:20:00
steps.instruction: Incubate the tube On ice for 00:20:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge the tube at 1000 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tube at 1000 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 to precipitate the pellet.

steps.title: Rinse the pellet with 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Rinse the pellet with 70% ethanol. Centrifuge the tube and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Air dry the pellet
steps.instruction: Air dry the pellet. Dissolve the pellet in 700 µL TE. Transfer DNA in TE to a new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube. Tap the tube gently. Wait until the pellet is completely dissolved. DNA can be stored at this step for overnight at 4 °C .

steps.title: Add RNase A into DNA solution...
steps.instruction: Add RNase A into DNA solution at a final concentration of 50 µg/ml . Mix well.

steps.title: Incubate the Eppendorf tube at 37 °C for 00:30:00
steps.instruction: Incubate the Eppendorf tube at 37 °C for 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Add 700 µL Tris-saturated Phenol
steps.instruction: Add 700 µL Tris-saturated Phenol. Mix thoroughly.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 16
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 16.000 rcf, °C, for 00:05:00 at Room temperature to separate phases.

steps.title: Transfer the upper phase to a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the upper phase to a new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube. Leave white stuff in the middle layer between two phases. Discard the middle and lower phases.

steps.title: Repeat steps 33-35 to extract DNA...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 33-35 to extract DNA in phenol one more time.

steps.title: After transferring the upper supernatant to...
steps.instruction: After transferring the upper supernatant to a new tube, add an equal volume of phenol: chloroform to the new tube. Mix thoroughly.

steps.title: Centrifuge the Eppendorf tube at 16
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the Eppendorf tube at 16.000 rcf, °C, for 00:03:00 at Room temperature to separate phases.

steps.title: Transfer the upper phase to a new Eppendorf tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the upper phase to a new Eppendorf tube. Discard the pellet.

steps.title: Centrifuge the tube at 16
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tube at 16.000 rcf, °C, for 00:03:00 at Room temperature to separate phases.

steps.title: Repeat steps 40-42 once to remove the phenol residue
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 40-42 once to remove the phenol residue.

steps.title: Add 1/10 volume of 3 Molarity...
steps.instruction: Add 1/10 volume of 3 Molarity (M) sodium acetate and 2x volume of pre-chilled 100% ethanol to the supernatant. Mix well.

steps.title: Incubate the tube On ice for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Incubate the tube On ice for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge the tube at 16
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tube at 16.000 rcf, °C, for 00:05:00 to collet the pellet. Discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Centrifuge the tube at 16
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tube at 16.000 rcf, °C, for 00:05:00 . Discard the ethanol. Pellet may not be attached tightly to the bottom of the Eppendorf tube. Be careful to pour off the supernatant.

steps.title: Air-dry the pellet by leaving the...
steps.instruction: Air-dry the pellet by leaving the Eppendorf tube in the fume hood. Wait until ethanol residue evaporated, but not over-dry the pellet.

steps.title: Dissolve the pellet in 100 µL TE
steps.instruction: Dissolve the pellet in 100 µL TE. Tap to dissolve genomic DNA.

steps.title: Weigh 2 g – 5 g...
steps.instruction: Weigh 2 g – 5 g fresh weight of leafy gametophytes.

steps.title: Pat dry leafy gametophytes with clean towel papers
steps.instruction: Pat dry leafy gametophytes with clean towel papers.

steps.title: Add 700 µL chloroform
steps.instruction: Add 700 µL chloroform: isoamyl alcohol to the new tube. Mix thoroughly.

steps.title: Transfer the aqueous supernatant to a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the aqueous supernatant to a new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Add 1 mL 70% ethanol to wash the pellet
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL 70% ethanol to wash the pellet. Finger tap the tube to mix.

steps.title: Incubate the conical tube On ice for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Incubate the conical tube On ice for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Add 400 µL 3 Molarity (M)...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL 3 Molarity (M) sodium acetate and 8 mL 100% pre-chilled ethanol to the new tube. Mix well.

steps.title: The entire procedure takes about 4 hours
steps.instruction: The entire procedure takes about 4 hours.

steps.title: The yield and quality of gDNA...
steps.instruction: The yield and quality of gDNA from four preps are listed below. FW, fresh weight. Table

steps.title: The yield and the quality of genomic DNA
steps.instruction: The yield and the quality of genomic DNA. %s %s Plant Tissue Extraction Method Yield (μg/g FW) OD 260/280 OD 260/230WTGametophytesThe modified CTABmethod69.21±8.43 1.95 ± 0.03 2.21 ± 0.12WTGametophytesNucleoSpin PlantDNA extraction Kit5.81±0.70 1.99 ± 0.02 2.20 ± 0.05

steps.title: The integrity of the extracted DNA
steps.instruction: The integrity of the extracted DNA. Genomic DNA isolated from WT (lane

steps.title: and the mutant (lane
steps.instruction: and the mutant (lane

steps.title: were in high quality
steps.instruction: were in high quality. WT DNA digested with BsaI (lane

steps.title: and ScaI (lane 4), together with...
steps.instruction: and ScaI (lane 4), together with mutant DNA digested with BsaI (lane

steps.title: and ScaI (lane 7), suggesting that...
steps.instruction: and ScaI (lane 7), suggesting that the isolated genomic DNA were free of endonuclease inhibitors. Lane 1, Invitrogen 1 kb plus DNA ladder. WT, wild type.
